title: How Insurance And Pharma Conspire Against Your Health (ft. Brigham Buhler)

chunks.title: Introduction to Healthcare Corruption
chunks.summary: The discussion begins with a stark portrayal of the corruption within the healthcare system in America, particularly focusing on big Pharma and big Insurance. The speaker emphasizes the need to uncover the root causes of chronic diseases and introduces a guest who is well-versed in exposing these corruptions.
chunks.transcript: the number one reason for bankruptcy in America is healthc care costs it's so corrupt it's killing people if you think that big Pharma or big Insurance really care about you you're wrong they are literally profiteering off of death and disease the only way we're really going to fix this broken Health Care system is to find out what's really causing this chronic disease crisis on this week's podcast I talked to my good friend Brigham buer you may have seen him on Rogan three times he just went on Tucker Carlson podcast talks about Pharmacy benefit managers Bigham knows the ways of insurance companies and he is out there exposing corruption in a huge way we need to protect this man he's also very knowledgeable on the history of pharmaceutical companies the corruption in those and the interplay between insurance and pharmaceutical companies and really how this is making Americans very sick it starts off in this podcast with a very dark message there is a lot of corruption happening within these industries but during the podcast we also present tools to evade this corruption to actually try and take back your healthare at insurance and pharmaceutical levels and give messages of Hope in terms of food and interventions like stem cells which are just on the horizon bringham is doing all sorts of great stuff now in Austin and broadly by creating cash pay services like ways to well helping people get access to healthcare he's an amazing human enjoy my podcast with

chunks.title: The Role of Pharmacy Benefit Managers
chunks.summary: The segment dives into the role of Pharmacy Benefit Managers (PBMs) in the healthcare system, established to negotiate drug costs but now purported to inflate prices and profit from chronic illnesses. The conversation highlights how these entities have been purchased by large insurance companies, further complicating the healthcare landscape.

chunks.title: Insurance Company Tactics
chunks.summary: The conversation progresses to discuss the tactics used by insurance companies to deny and delay care, effectively profiting from chronic disease management rather than prevention. A significant focus is placed on the detrimental outcomes stemming from these practices and how they impact patients seeking care.

chunks.title: Chronic Diseases and Preventative Measures
chunks.summary: This segment emphasizes that most chronic diseases are preventable with lifestyle changes and diet. The speakers argue for the necessity of proactive healthcare approaches and the responsibilities of both healthcare providers and patients in addressing diet and lifestyle choices.

chunks.title: Role of Nutrition in Health
chunks.summary: The speakers highlight the significance of nutrition and dietary choices in preventative health care. They discuss how processed foods contribute to chronic conditions and stress the importance of returning to whole, nutrient-rich foods to improve community health outcomes.

chunks.title: Accountability and Empowerment in Health
chunks.summary: This segment reinforces the notion of personal accountability in health, urging individuals to take charge of their health decisions. The conversation promotes educational approaches to foster better lifestyle choices and the necessity of addressing societal and systemic issues that hinder access to healthy options.

chunks.title: Conclusion and Call to Action
chunks.summary: The podcast concludes with a strong call to action for listeners to stay informed, prioritize their health, and advocate for necessary changes within the healthcare system. The speakers reiterate the importance of collective efforts to drive meaningful reforms in health care.
